reviews.review_msg: Robin is the best prof I've had. He makes each class engaging and tries teaching the concept in the easiest way for students to understand. He promotes asking questions and always explains any answer in detail even staying late every class to answer questions as well. Had him in Bus 320 and 321it was honestly a blessing. He's honestly the man

One of my fav profs but his class is hard. I scored ~20% below on the midterm due to my incompetence, but bounced near the highest mark on the final. Lectures are fast, but he goes over problems in lectures. Doesn't make sense during lecture but if you review it later + take detailed notes + GPT + do HW, it'll get better. He's like a mean dad tho..
Hes an interesting critter.

An erudite accounting professor with fast-paced yet lucid lectures. But, most people can't finish his exams and the average for fall 2025 is 50%. 

Exam tips: focus on syllabus questions, midterm and final mirror textbook “P” problems (not “BE” or “E”), especially Excel-related ones. 
Pre-read chapters.
Record lectures with his permission.

I felt like I paid $500 for a lousy comedy show and 20 bullets to my GPA. Lectures were off-topic, and when he did teach, it was so fast I couldn't keep up. Robin was very chummy with a select group of students but dismissive when I tried to talk with him. This made me feel uncomfortable reaching out for help with the material.
good prof and cares about his students! tests are hard. he is hot tho

By far my favorite accounting professor so far. I took him for both BUS 320 and BUS 321, and I always look forward to his lectures; they're informative, engaging, and even fun. Both courses are naturally challenging, but if you stay on top of the material, you'll do great. I highly recommend taking Robin if you get the chance!

Entertaining and engaging classes but the exams are completely out of control. The difficulty spike is far beyond the practice questions in the slides and the excel questions. If I wanted to be entertained I would go to a circus and not an accounting course.
Not a considerate person. Does not care about his students. Conceited for no reason.
He is making one of the hardest courses very engaging. Super responsive and kind. The best part that he is a nice person, which is a rare thing at SFU. Take Robin if you can!

Robin is funny, so lectures are engaging, but the weekly political tangents get tedious. Homework is easier than the exam, which he tells you the questions for—but they're tricky, so do lots of textbook practice. Reading the textbook isn't very helpful, and the slides are quite simple, so it is hard to catch-up if you miss a lecture.

Robin's lectures are very engaging (although he does enjoy talking about politics...)-- his in-class practice examples are well-thought out, and it's clear that he knows what he is doing. Also, a genuinely nice guy. Would recommend!

Very test heavy; to do well, do as many practice questions as you can, and be prepared for tricky details on tests!
Fun. Enjoyable. Actually enjoyed his class and participate all class although participation not mandatory. Strongly recommend.

Exams are pretty tough and very time-constrained with low averages, but he will tell you what type of questions will be on the exams. In order to do well stay on top of practice Q's, Wiley HW, and pre-readings. Overall 10/10 guy. Hilarious and good lecturer.

Robin is a terrific prof! He is super entertaining and covers all material in a clear manner. Exams are definitely tough and time-constrained, so lots of practice is required. Make sure to do and understand all the homework and self-study problems. He gives you the exact format of the exam, so focus only on what he mentions. Textbook reading helps!
Wondering how he can give the same % marks for all the assignments in a course. Not sure if he pays attention to the details.
Read directly from Slides

Exams are easy if you do all the assignments on your own and pay attention in class. He can be a bit unclear when explaining certain concepts due to his accents or what not but overall he does try hard to help you understand. A nice and fair prof
Exams are not very hard if you do all the assignments. His lectures will help you understand many concepts and he teaches some tricks and some techniques which is good. Very good prof overall.
GOOD
Love him
He is the best Acc prof at SFU.

Just a disclaimer that Richard didn't teach 321 when I took it. However, his course structure, learning materials, and lecture notes were still used, and it made learning a difficult course much more tolerable and even enjoyable sometimes. He didn't teach it, but he clearly made his mark!
Go off topic in class and no organized notes, the test is quiet different from what learned in class..and he made everything so complicated to understand

Kim is a very nice professor and for the most part, she can get across the necessary material. It can be a difficult course because she stumbles and can confuse herself while lecturing, doing all the questions and teaching yourself is the best way to learn along with the examples you do in class. Luckily if you show up to lectures you get 10%.

Excellent professor. He is technical but also encourages his students to think outside the box. He cares about his students and is very responsive to requests for help. Best accounting professor I have had.

One of the hardest accounting class in SFU. He tried his best to explain things and gave students good practices to prepare for the exams. His lecture notes are gems, included everything you need to know, However, there are so many things, so it is hard to remember all of them for the exams. He seems to be the best prof we have for BUS321 :)
He is one of the best accounting pofs I've ever had. He is a great marker and really helpful. He always responds to my emails quickly even after two years. He is amazing.
If there is a way to make accounting fun, Jamal knows how to do it.  His pace was sometimes very slow and sometimes a bit too fast but that is true for most courses anyway.

Best 321 professor hands down. Has great notes, basically a summary of the textbook. His exams are very fair- exactly what the HWQ and Notes are.   It's a difficult course, and he makes it manageable. I can't imagine taking this class with any other professor.
He is a great prof who can give a good understanding of fundamental principles in accounting. Really knowledgeable. Exams are pretty hard though and preparations require tons of effort.
Very good prof. Study his lecture well. Exam can be hard but the class is curved anyway. Going to class is a must to succeed. Reading textbook is unnecessary.
Just as fast as finish the lecture (only read slides), not caring about if students totally understand the concepts or not.

He seems very nice and laid back. He arranged a deal with us to start class 15min. later which is fine, however quite often he was more than 15min. late to start class, and somehow would manage to finish class 30mins. early! Though he knows his stuff, he goes through the lectures VERY quickly. If you like to learn on your own, take his class.
Great prof to take accounting with. He is very friendly and helpful, so come to his office hours often, you will learn a lot from him
Great prof! He truly cares about his students and does his best to deliver the accounting materiel. would recommend / 10

Worst prof ever. The guy doesn't teach. He uses the publisher's ppt and just reads off of it. If you're unlucky enough to have him, just skip class and learn the material yourself, it'll be a better use of your 3 hours.
